details: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Mahalakshmi Kit Yojana" has been launched by the Department of Women Empowerment & Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to improve the health and well-being of both mothers and their newborn girl children by providing essential item kits after the institutional delivery of pregnant women registered at an Anganwadi Centre.
benefits: In the state, after the institutional delivery of a pregnant woman, the following benefits will be provided:
The woman will receive one Mahalakshmi kit for the birth of each of her first two girl children.
In the case of twins, the woman will be given one Mahalakshmi kit, and each child will receive a separate Mahalakshmi kit.
eligibility: The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
The beneficiary must be a pregnant woman registered with an Anganwadi Centre and eligible to receive a Mahalakshmi Kit after delivery.
The beneficiary must not be an income tax payer.

offline: After the institutional delivery of a pregnant woman registered at an Anganwadi Centre, a Mahalakshmi Kit is provided as a benefit.
The following items are included in the kit for mothers and their newborn girl children:
Dry fruits, socks, scarf, towel, shawl, blanket, bedsheet, sanitary pads, mustard oil, soap, and nail cutter.
Cotton/warm cap, socks, diaper, towel, baby soap, rubber sheet, warm blanket, and vaccination card.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Pregnant women registered with an Anganwadi Centre, who are not income tax payers, are eligible to receive the Mahalakshmi Kit after delivery.

faqs.question: Where should a pregnant woman be registered to avail benefits?
faqs.answer: She must be registered at an Anganwadi Centre in Uttarakhand.

faqs.question: When is the Mahalakshmi Kit provided?
faqs.answer: The kit is provided after the institutional delivery of the pregnant woman.

faqs.question: How many kits does a woman receive?
faqs.answer: A woman receives one kit for each of her first two girl children.

faqs.question: What happens in the case of twins?
faqs.answer: For twins, the woman receives one Mahalakshmi Kit, and each child is given a separate Mahalakshmi Kit.

faqs.question: Can women who are income tax payers receive the kit?
faqs.answer: No, only women who are not income tax payers are eligible.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available for mothers of boy children?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is specifically for mothers of girl children.

faqs.question: Is registration at the Anganwadi Centre mandatory?
faqs.answer: Yes, only women registered at an Anganwadi Centre are eligible for the kit.

faqs.question: Does the kit support home deliveries?
faqs.answer: No, the kit is provided only after institutional delivery at recognized health facilities.
